verses.text: Solomon's proverbs begin: a wise son gladdens his father, but a foolish one grieves his mother; ill-gotten treasure profits nothing, while God will not let the righteous starve. Character shows its fruit. God feeds the upright and frustrates the wicked's gains.

Solomon's proverbs begin: a wise son gladdens his father, but a foolish one grieves his mother; ill-gotten treasure profits nothing, while God will not let the righteous starve. Character shows its fruit. God feeds the upright and frustrates the wicked's gains.

Solomon's proverbs begin: a wise son gladdens his father, but a foolish one grieves his mother; ill-gotten treasure profits nothing, while God will not let the righteous starve. Character shows its fruit. God feeds the upright and frustrates the wicked's gains.

A slack hand brings poverty, but the diligent hand makes rich; he who gathers in summer is wise. Industry is honoured. Blessings rest on the head of the just, while violence covers the mouth of the wicked.

A slack hand brings poverty, but the diligent hand makes rich; he who gathers in summer is wise. Industry is honoured. Blessings rest on the head of the just, while violence covers the mouth of the wicked.

A slack hand brings poverty, but the diligent hand makes rich; he who gathers in summer is wise. Industry is honoured. Blessings rest on the head of the just, while violence covers the mouth of the wicked.

'The memory of the just is blessed,' but the wicked's name shall rot; the wise heed commands, and 'he that walketh uprightly walketh surely.' Integrity is safe. A straight life leaves a good name and a steady footing.

'The memory of the just is blessed,' but the wicked's name shall rot; the wise heed commands, and 'he that walketh uprightly walketh surely.' Integrity is safe. A straight life leaves a good name and a steady footing.

'The memory of the just is blessed,' but the wicked's name shall rot; the wise heed commands, and 'he that walketh uprightly walketh surely.' Integrity is safe. A straight life leaves a good name and a steady footing.

Mischievous gestures and prating fools bring sorrow and fall, but 'love covereth all sins.' Where hatred stirs strife, love forgives and heals. The loving heart conceals and pardons rather than exposes.

Mischievous gestures and prating fools bring sorrow and fall, but 'love covereth all sins.' Where hatred stirs strife, love forgives and heals. The loving heart conceals and pardons rather than exposes.

Mischievous gestures and prating fools bring sorrow and fall, but 'love covereth all sins.' Where hatred stirs strife, love forgives and heals. The loving heart conceals and pardons rather than exposes.

Wisdom is found in the lips of the discerning, but a rod awaits the foolish; the rich man's wealth is his stronghold, the poor man's poverty his ruin. Words reveal the heart. The wise speak helpfully; fools store up only trouble.

Wisdom is found in the lips of the discerning, but a rod awaits the foolish; the rich man's wealth is his stronghold, the poor man's poverty his ruin. Words reveal the heart. The wise speak helpfully; fools store up only trouble.

Wisdom is found in the lips of the discerning, but a rod awaits the foolish; the rich man's wealth is his stronghold, the poor man's poverty his ruin. Words reveal the heart. The wise speak helpfully; fools store up only trouble.

The righteous man's labour leads to life, the wicked's to sin; he who heeds instruction is in the way of life, but he who hides hatred with lying lips is a fool. Conduct has consequences. Honest correction is better than concealed malice.

The righteous man's labour leads to life, the wicked's to sin; he who heeds instruction is in the way of life, but he who hides hatred with lying lips is a fool. Conduct has consequences. Honest correction is better than concealed malice.

The righteous man's labour leads to life, the wicked's to sin; he who heeds instruction is in the way of life, but he who hides hatred with lying lips is a fool. Conduct has consequences. Honest correction is better than concealed malice.

'In the multitude of words there wanteth not sin,' but the prudent restrain their lips; the tongue of the just is choice silver, feeding many. Few and wise words bless. The righteous nourish others, while fools die for lack of sense.

'In the multitude of words there wanteth not sin,' but the prudent restrain their lips; the tongue of the just is choice silver, feeding many. Few and wise words bless. The righteous nourish others, while fools die for lack of sense.

'In the multitude of words there wanteth not sin,' but the prudent restrain their lips; the tongue of the just is choice silver, feeding many. Few and wise words bless. The righteous nourish others, while fools die for lack of sense.

'The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich,' adding no sorrow with it; the wicked's fear shall come upon him, but the desire of the righteous is granted. True wealth is God's gift. What He gives carries no hidden grief.

'The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich,' adding no sorrow with it; the wicked's fear shall come upon him, but the desire of the righteous is granted. True wealth is God's gift. What He gives carries no hidden grief.

'The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich,' adding no sorrow with it; the wicked's fear shall come upon him, but the desire of the righteous is granted. True wealth is God's gift. What He gives carries no hidden grief.

As the whirlwind passes, the wicked are gone, but the righteous have an everlasting foundation; 'the fear of the LORD prolongeth days.' The godly endure. Reverence for God adds length and stability to life.

As the whirlwind passes, the wicked are gone, but the righteous have an everlasting foundation; 'the fear of the LORD prolongeth days.' The godly endure. Reverence for God adds length and stability to life.

As the whirlwind passes, the wicked are gone, but the righteous have an everlasting foundation; 'the fear of the LORD prolongeth days.' The godly endure. Reverence for God adds length and stability to life.

'The hope of the righteous shall be gladness,' but the expectation of the wicked perishes; the righteous shall never be removed, but the wicked shall not dwell in the land. Two hopes, two ends. The just are rooted; the wicked are swept away.

'The hope of the righteous shall be gladness,' but the expectation of the wicked perishes; the righteous shall never be removed, but the wicked shall not dwell in the land. Two hopes, two ends. The just are rooted; the wicked are swept away.

'The hope of the righteous shall be gladness,' but the expectation of the wicked perishes; the righteous shall never be removed, but the wicked shall not dwell in the land. Two hopes, two ends. The just are rooted; the wicked are swept away.

The mouth of the just brings forth wisdom, but the froward tongue is cut out; the lips of the righteous 'know what is acceptable,' while the wicked speak frowardness. Speech reveals the soul. The godly weigh their words; the wicked pour out only perverseness.

The mouth of the just brings forth wisdom, but the froward tongue is cut out; the lips of the righteous 'know what is acceptable,' while the wicked speak frowardness. Speech reveals the soul. The godly weigh their words; the wicked pour out only perverseness.
